vulnerabilities.description: The server validates submitted URLs by making server-side requests. Posting url=http://127.0.0.1:5003/ returned: "URL status was checked and returned 200 status", confirming access to localhost/internal resources.
The URL is passed to a shell command. Submitting url=http://example.com";sleep 5;# produced a ~5s response delay, indicating command execution via shell injection.
State-changing POST endpoint accepts form data without any CSRF token; direct curl POST succeeds, indicating likely CSRF vulnerability.
Destructive POST endpoint has no visible CSRF protection and accepts requests without tokens.
Deletion is unauthenticated and ID-based; any unauthenticated user can attempt to delete arbitrary entries by specifying IDs.
